name: Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B A55B
license: OpenMDW License Agreement v1.1 (Linux Foundation Model Openness Framework)
archDisplay: Hybrid LatentMoE + Mamba-2 (550B total / 55B active, MTP layers, NVFP4-native pretraining)
note: NVIDIA's frontier open MoE with 1M-token context; hybrid LatentMoE+Mamba-2 flagship of the Nemotron 3 family, tuned for long-running agents.
tpsNote: Needs 8x GB200/B200/H200-class GPUs natively; community GGUF still needs ~300GB+ combined RAM/VRAM, far beyond a single RTX 4090.

results.notes: Agent productivity benchmark [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The official technical report states PinchBench = 90.0 for the BF16 model in three separate places: the main model-comparison table (Table 10), the quantization-comparison table, and explicit prose ('On PinchBench, Nemotron 3 Ultra achieves 90.0, within 1.3 points of the best model in the comparison'). The existing 91 figure, per the corpus's own verificationNotes, came from secondary blog coverag]

Instruction following [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The technical report's main comparison table (Table 10) reports IFBench (prompt loose) = 81.7 for the BF16 model, consistent with the MOPD-ablation table's final checkpoint value of 81.7 elsewhere in the same paper. The existing corpus figure of 82 (sourced from the developer blog) rounds/paraphrases the primary source's precise number.]
Table 10, HLE no-tools; with-tools variant scores 37.4.

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 21/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "Nemotron 3 Ultra" (NVIDIA). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting. SealedBench 'Nemotron 3 Ultra' is this corpus's 'Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B A55B'.

verificationNotes:
This is the newest/highest-risk record but is genuinely real and well-corroborated: confirmed via direct HF model-card fetch (550B total/55B active, OpenMDW-1.1 license, 1M ctx, released 2026-06-04) plus independent corroboration from NVIDIA's developer blog, research.nvidia.com, MarkTechPost, Linux Foundation press release on OpenMDW-1.1, and OpenRouter (pricing $0.50/$2.20 matched exactly). Benchmark rows MMLU-Pro 86.8, SWE-Bench Verified 70.7, and LiveCodeBench v6 89.0 confirmed on the HF card; RULER@1M 94.7 and IFBench 82 confirmed via NVIDIA's blog and secondary coverage; PinchBench score varies slightly across secondary sources (90 vs 91) but 91 is corroborated by at least one source matching the record.

2026-07-16: Checked all 5 missing core benchmarks (MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, MMMU) against (1) the official technical report (research.nvidia.com PDF, all 65 pages full-text extracted and grepped for every variant of these names), the HF model card, the NVIDIA developer blog, and build.nvidia.com; (2) independent leaderboards Artificial Analysis (Intelligence Index v4.1's 9 components) and Vals.ai's model page; (3) the sibling Nemotron 3 Super technical report (arXiv 2604.12374) for cross-reference. None of the five are published for the post-trained/BF16 model this record tracks: the report's Table 10 (post-trained comparison), the HF card, and the dev blog all use MMLU-Pro, MMLU-ProX, IMOAnswerBench, MathArena Apex-Shortlist, and LiveCodeBench v6 in their place — a deliberate move away from largely-saturated legacy benchmarks (notably, the immediately-prior Nemotron 3 Super report still reported AIME25=90.21, so the Ultra report visibly dropped AIME in favor of harder olympiad-style evals). The report's separate Table 2 does list MMLU=89.08 (5-shot), MATH/Minerva-4-shot=82.00, and HumanEval(EvalPlus)=83.84, but those are explicitly scored for 'Nemotron-3-Ultra-550B-A55B-Base' — the pretrained base checkpoint, a different artifact from the post-trained BF16 release tracked in this record — so they were deliberately not carried over here to avoid base/post-trained variant conflation. MMMU is additionally structurally inapplicable: this model is text-only (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false), so no multimodal benchmark score exists or would be expected. All five gaps are therefore genuine, expected absences, not omissions to fix.

2026-07-17 (round 2, deep pass on legacy MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval gaps): Checked 6 source types not touched by the 2026-07-16 pass. (1) Stanford HELM (capabilities/latest, lite/latest) — JS-rendered SPA blocked static fetch of table data, but exhaustive web searches for 'Nemotron 3 Ultra' + HELM surfaced zero leaderboard entries; this model (released 2026-06-04) does not appear to be in HELM's eval queue yet. (2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar — pulled the live citation graph for the official Ultra paper (arXiv 2606.15007) via the Semantic Scholar API: 3 citing papers (speculative-decoding study 2607.08690, 'Red Queen Gödel Machine' 2606.26294, 'Positive Alignment' 2605.10310), none report MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval scores for this model — they cite it only as an inference/drafting backend or in unrelated alignment-philosophy discussion. Also checked the sibling compression paper 'Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B-A9B' (arXiv 2607.04371), which compresses Nemotron-3-Super, not Ultra. (3) GitHub eval-harness logs — found NVIDIA's own NVIDIA-NeMo/Evaluator repo with a dedicated nemotron-3-ultra reproducibility guide and the actual YAML task configs run against the post-trained checkpoint (v0.2/local_nemotron-3-ultra-550b-a55b.yaml, v0.3 configs). This independent, non-marketing source confirms the configured task list (ns_gpqa, ns_hle_aa, ns_mmlu_pro, ns_mmlu_prox, ns_scicode, ns_critpt, ns_ifbench, ns_aa_lcr, ns_omniscience, ns_imo_answerbench, multi-challenge, ns_wmt24pp_comet, plus LiveCodeBench v6/Tau2/GDPVal/SWE-bench/Terminal-bench variants) deliberately excludes MMLU, MATH, AIME, and HumanEval for the post-trained BF16 model — corroborating the prior round's conclusion at the eval-config level, not just from paper prose. No results.json with actual numbers is committed to the repo (it's a config/reproducibility guide only). (4) Other leaderboards — OpenRouter's model page has no independent numbers beyond deferring to BenchLM/Artificial Analysis (both already checked in round 1); Scale AI SEAL leaderboard is JS-rendered and inaccessible, and its four public tracks (code-gen, Spanish, instruction-following, 5th-grade math) aren't the benchmarks in question regardless; no PapersWithCode leaderboard entry exists for this model. (5) Non-English sources — checked Chinese (Baidu Baike, CNMO, QQ News, Zhihu) and Korean coverage; found only a paraphrased/inconsistent MMLU-Pro restatement (one Chinese AI-search summary gave 89.5 vs. the corpus's own primary-sourced 86.8, indicating an unreliable secondary source), no vanilla MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers anywhere. (6) Sibling models — checked the Nemotron 3 Super technical report (arXiv 2604.12374), which predates Ultra's release and therefore cannot and does not include Ultra as a comparison point; the Puzzle-75B-A9B paper compresses Super, not Ultra. All 6 new source types were genuinely exhausted with no credible citable score found for any of the four gaps; no corpus changes made.

2026-07-18 (MMMU gap check): Re-confirmed the model's own corpus fields before searching further, per protocol: modalities: ["text"] (single entry, no "image"/"vision" modality listed) and capabilities.vision: false, both set explicitly in this record and corroborated by the HF model card (nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Ultra-550B-A55B-BF16), the NVIDIA developer blog launch post, and the official technical report (research.nvidia.com PDF) — none of which describe any image/vision input pathway, vision encoder, or multimodal projector for this checkpoint; the architecture is described purely as "Hybrid LatentMoE + Mamba-2" text-only. NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 family does not ship a released vision-language variant paired with the Ultra 550B-A55B text checkpoint (unlike some other frontier labs' dual text/VLM releases), so there is no sibling multimodal artifact under this exact name to check either. MMMU (Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding) requires image-grounded question answering, which is architecturally impossible to evaluate on a model with no vision input capability — this is not a case of the benchmark being unpublished/not-yet-reported (unlike, e.g., a vision-capable model simply not having run the eval), it is a structural mismatch between benchmark requirements and model capabilities. No further searching across independent trackers (Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, Artificial Analysis, llm-stats.com), academic citations, or GitHub eval-harness logs was performed for MMMU specifically, consistent with the protocol's instruction to stop once text-only/no-vision is confirmed from the model's own capability fields. Conclusion: MMMU remains correctly absent from this record; omitted_no_evidence with reason "architecturally inapplicable (text-only model)".
